refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement period and baseline, (2) what constitutes 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., does safety-driven shutdown count as unavailability?), (3) which subsystems or failure modes dominate unavailability, and (4) the allocation of availability targets across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services domains. High safety criticality means that safety-driven shutdowns must be distinguished from reliability failures.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational telemetry (uptime/downtime logs, failure modes, maintenance events) over the agreed evaluation period. Calculate availability as (Total Time − Unplanned Downtime − Planned Maintenance) / Total Time. Stratify by subsystem (Vehicle propulsion, Teleoperation link, Safety Services) to identify bottlenecks. Compare against customer-defined target and acceptance threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.98 availability (propulsion, steering, hydraulics, sensors).
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (network link, command latency, video feed).
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (hazard detection, emergency stop, safety alerts).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and predictive maintenance alerts to enable proactive intervention.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy and fault tolerance across all critical subsystems (Vehicle drivetrain, Teleoperation network, Safety Services sensors) with automated failover and health monitoring.
DP-B: Establish a preventive maintenance schedule based on component wear models and operational hours, with spare parts inventory and rapid replacement protocols.

DP-C: Deploy a centralized availability monitoring and analytics platform that ingests telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, calculates real-time availability KPI, and triggers maintenance alerts.
DP-D: Implement a hybrid approach combining redundancy (DP-A), preventive maintenance (DP-B), and monitoring (DP-C) with clear ownership and escalation paths.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a hybrid availability assurance strategy combining: (1) subsystem-level redundancy and fault tolerance (Vehicle drivetrain backup, Teleoperation dual-link failover, Safety Services sensor voting), (2) predictive maintenance based on component health models and operational telemetry, and (3) a centralized availability monitoring platform that calculates KPI in real-time, logs failure modes, and triggers maintenance workflows. Ownership is distributed: Vehicle owns drivetrain redundancy, Teleoperation owns network failover, Safety Services owns sensor voting, and NGHE Solution (system level) owns the monitoring platform and KPI calculation.

potential_sources: Telemetry interface must carry health/status data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to the monitoring platform; this creates a dependency on consistent telemetry schema and latency requirements.
Failover logic in Teleoperation and Vehicle must be coordinated to avoid conflicting state transitions during simultaneous failures.
Maintenance scheduling must account for safety-driven shutdowns (Safety Services) vs. reliability-driven downtime (Vehicle/Teleoperation) to avoid double-counting unavailability.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is moderately coupled because it requires integration across three subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and a system-level monitoring platform. However, the coupling is manageable via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and clear ownership boundaries. The risk is that poor telemetry schema or monitoring logic could mask subsystem failures.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Redundancy components (backup drivetrain, dual network links, sensor voting) are available and cost-effective within project budget.
ASSUMPTION: Telemetry data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services can be reliably collected and transmitted to the monitoring platform with <5s latency.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive maintenance models (e.g., component wear curves) are validated and applicable to the NGHE operating environment.
RISK: If redundancy is not fully implemented (e.g., only Vehicle has backup, but Teleoperation does not), the overall availability target may not be achievable.
RISK: If telemetry is incomplete or delayed, the monitoring platform may not detect failures in time to trigger preventive action.
RISK: Safety-driven shutdowns (e.g., emergency stop triggered by hazard detection) may be incorrectly classified as 'unavailability' rather than 'safe operation,' skewing the KPI.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the definition of 'unavailability' to exclude safety-driven shutdowns and planned maintenance windows. Allocate specific availability targets to Vehicle (≥0.98), Teleoperation (≥0.99), and Safety Services (≥0.99) to guide subsystem design. Specify the telemetry schema, latency requirements, and monitoring platform architecture in the DP to reduce ambiguity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability monitoring, KPI calculation, and orchestration of redundancy/failover across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Includes the centralized monitoring platform, analytics engine, and maintenance workflow integration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives health/status data from Vehicle (drivetrain, hydraulics, sensors), Teleoperation (network link quality, latency, command success rate), and Safety Services (sensor health, alert frequency). Used to calculate real-time availability KPI and detect anomalies.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical alerts from Safety Services (e.g., hazard detected, emergency stop triggered). Used to classify downtime as safety-driven vs. reliability-driven and to trigger coordinated failover if needed.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends maintenance task assignments and failover commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Coordinates redundancy activation and component replacement scheduling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with remote monitoring/analytics backend (if cloud-based) to store telemetry, run predictive models, and provide dashboards to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of availability KPI calculation, monitoring platform, and cross-subsystem orchestration. Monitoring logic is centralized here; subsystem-specific redundancy (Vehicle drivetrain, Teleoperation failover) is owned by respective subsystems but coordinated by NGHE Solution. Low diffusion risk because monitoring platform is a cohesive artifact.

Vehicle owns drivetrain redundancy and local health monitoring, but availability KPI calculation and cross-subsystem failover orchestration belong at NGHE Solution level. If availability logic is scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, diffusion risk is high.

Teleoperation owns network failover and link quality monitoring, but system-level KPI and maintenance orchestration belong at NGHE Solution level. Moderate containment because Teleoperation is a critical availability bottleneck but not the sole owner of the KPI.

Safety Services owns sensor voting and hazard detection, which contribute to availability. However, Safety Services is primarily responsible for safety, not availability KPI. Availability monitoring must distinguish safety-driven shutdowns from reliability failures, which is a system-level concern.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The availability monitoring platform, KPI calculation engine, and cross-subsystem failover orchestration are best contained within NGHE Solution as a cohesive system-level module. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective redundancy and health monitoring, but they report telemetry and safety alerts to NGHE Solution, which synthesizes the KPI and triggers maintenance/failover actions. This avoids diffusion of availability logic across multiple subsystems.
containment_risks: If telemetry schema is not standardized, each subsystem may report health data in different formats, requiring translation logic in NGHE Solution and increasing complexity.

If failover logic is not clearly separated from subsystem-specific redundancy, there is risk of conflicting state transitions (e.g., Vehicle activates backup drivetrain while Teleoperation switches to secondary network link, causing coordination issues).
If availability KPI calculation is not transparent and auditable, stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager) may not trust the reported availability metric.
If monitoring platform is cloud-based and Network Link fails, the system loses visibility into availability, creating a single point of failure.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a standardized telemetry schema (e.g., JSON with fields: subsystem_id, component_id, health_status, timestamp, failure_mode). Specify failover orchestration logic as a state machine in NGHE Solution to coordinate redundancy activation across subsystems. Implement local availability monitoring in Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services as a fallback if Network Link to cloud backend is unavailable. Provide audit logs and dashboards to Site Manager and Maintenance teams to verify KPI calculation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid availability assurance) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability) by combining redundancy, preventive maintenance, and monitoring. The DP is logically sound and feasible. However, the DP must be refined to specify subsystem-level availability targets (Vehicle ≥0.98, Teleoperation ≥0.99, Safety Services ≥0.99) to guide subsystem design and ensure the overall target is achievable.

diffusion_risks: Availability monitoring logic could be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if ownership boundaries are not clearly defined. Mitigation: Centralize KPI calculation in NGHE Solution; subsystems report telemetry only.

Failover orchestration could be duplicated if each subsystem implements its own failover logic without coordination. Mitigation: Define a single failover state machine in NGHE Solution; subsystems execute commands from NGHE Solution.
Telemetry schema could diverge if subsystems define their own health data formats. Mitigation: Enforce a standardized telemetry schema at the NGHE Solution level.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for availability monitoring and KPI calculation (containment_score 0.95). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own subsystem-specific redundancy and health monitoring but report to NGHE Solution. This avoids diffusion and maintains clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via operational telemetry collection and KPI calculation over the agreed evaluation period. Requires instrumentation of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to report health/status data to NGHE Solution monitoring platform. Requires definition of 'unavailability' (e.g., unplanned downtime, excluding planned maintenance and safety-driven shutdowns).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns system-level KPI and monitoring; Vehicle owns drivetrain redundancy (target ≥0.98); Teleoperation owns network failover (target ≥0.99); Safety Services owns sensor voting (target ≥0.99). However, the allocation must be documented in a traceability matrix and communicated to subsystem teams.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to specify subsystem-level availability targets and the telemetry schema.
Define 'unavailability' explicitly to exclude safety-driven shutdowns and planned maintenance.
Specify the monitoring platform architecture (centralized vs. distributed, cloud vs. on-premise).
Allocate availability targets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services in a formal requirements document.
Define failover orchestration logic as a state machine or decision tree in NGHE Solution.
Establish audit and verification procedures to ensure KPI calculation is transparent and auditable.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into subsystem-level availability targets and specific design solutions. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, but they are not atomic and require further decomposition. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) must be flowed down to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services teams to guide their design. The IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is appropriate for the monitoring platform, but subsystem-level IMs (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be assigned to the sub-FRs to ensure complete coverage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.98 availability (propulsion, steering, hydraulics, sensors).
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (network link, command latency, video feed).
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (hazard detection, emergency stop, safety alerts).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and predictive maintenance alerts to enable proactive intervention.

revise.why: DP-D is sound but requires refinement to specify: (1) subsystem-level availability targets, (2) telemetry schema and latency requirements, (3) failover orchestration logic, (4) definition of 'unavailability' (excluding safety-driven shutdowns), and (5) monitoring platform architecture. These refinements will guide subsystem design and reduce ambiguity.

assumptions: Redundancy components (backup drivetrain, dual network links, sensor voting) are available and cost-effective.
Telemetry data can be reliably collected and transmitted with <5s latency.
Predictive maintenance models are validated and applicable to NGHE operating environment.
Safety-driven shutdowns are distinguished from reliability-driven downtime in KPI calculation.
Subsystem teams (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will implement their respective availability targets and report telemetry to NGHE Solution.
open_risks: If redundancy is not fully implemented across all subsystems, overall availability target may not be achievable.
If telemetry is incomplete or delayed, monitoring platform may not detect failures in time.
If failover logic is not coordinated, conflicting state transitions could occur during simultaneous failures.
If monitoring platform is cloud-based and Network Link fails, system loses visibility into availability.
If availability KPI calculation is not transparent, stakeholders may not trust the metric.
evidence_needed: Subsystem-level availability targets and design solutions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
Telemetry schema and interface specifications (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link).
Failover orchestration logic and state machine definition.
Monitoring platform architecture and implementation plan.
Operational telemetry data and KPI calculation results from pilot or field trial.
Audit logs and dashboards demonstrating KPI transparency and auditability.
